sections.paragraph:
παρεισφέρω Hellenistic aor. παρεισήνεγκα (B-D-F §81, 2; Rob. 338) (Demosth. et al.; PTebt 38, 12; 14 [113 b.c.]) apply, bring to bear σπουδὴν πᾶσαν make every effort, do your best 2 Pt 1:5 (σπουδὴν [πᾶσαν] εἰσφέρειν is a formal expr. in the Koine in ref. to benefactors and other civic-minded pers: Polyb 21, 29, 12; Diod S 1, 83, 8; SIG [index s.v. σπουδή]; Jos., Ant. 20, 204).—M-M. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

expr. expr. = expression

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 774
